text.content:
⚔ Potence

Level 1: Lethal Body

Cost: Free | Pool: -- | Type: Combat

Passive. Your unarmed attacks (punches, kicks, bites) deal Aggravated damage to mortals instead of Superficial. Against vampires, your unarmed attacks still deal Superficial damage but cannot be halved (they are treated as if from a weapon). Your grip strength is similarly enhanced — you can crush metal cans, bend bars, and leave handprint indentations in wood.

Level 1: Soaring Leap

Cost: Free | Pool: -- | Type: Utility

Passive. You can jump extraordinary distances — vertically up to 3 meters per Potence dot, horizontally up to 5 meters per Potence dot, from a standing start. Running jumps double these distances. You always land safely regardless of height. This allows you to reach rooftops, clear fences, and leap between buildings. Witnesses will obviously notice something superhuman.

Level 2: Prowess

Cost: 1 Rouse | Pool: -- | Type: Combat

Add your Potence rating as bonus dice to all Strength-based rolls: melee damage, feats of strength (lifting, breaking, throwing), and grappling. Also add Potence as bonus Superficial damage to all melee attacks. With this active, you can flip cars, punch through concrete walls, and throw grown adults across rooms. Lasts one scene.

Level 3: Brutal Feed

Cost: Free | Pool: -- | Type: Combat

Passive. When you feed on a mortal, you can drain them completely in a single turn (normally takes several). The target takes 3 Aggravated damage from the savage feeding and is left in critical condition. You reduce your Hunger by 3 instead of the normal amount. The feeding is messy and violent — the victim is left with obvious wounds. Useful in emergencies but a severe Masquerade risk.

Level 3: Spark of Rage

Cost: 1 Rouse | Pool: Man + Potence vs Composure + Resolve | Type: Combat

Touch a target (requires a successful Brawl attack) and flood their Beast with rage. Mortals fly into an uncontrollable violent rage for the rest of the scene, attacking the nearest person. Vampires must immediately make a Frenzy check at Difficulty 4. Even on success, the vampire suffers a 2-dice penalty to all non-violent actions for the rest of the scene due to simmering rage. Cannot be used on the same target more than once per night.

Level 3: Uncanny Grip

Cost: Free | Pool: -- | Type: Utility

Passive. Cling to any surface — walls, ceilings, glass — with supernatural grip. You can walk vertically, hang upside down, or maintain position on a sheer cliff face with no roll required. You can support an additional person's weight while climbing. The grip cannot be broken by non-supernatural means. Useful for ambushes, infiltration, and dramatic entrances.

Level 4: Draught of Might

Cost: 1 Rouse | Pool: -- | Type: Utility

Feed a mortal or ghoul your blood to grant them Potence 1 (Lethal Body or Soaring Leap, your choice) for one night. The recipient must drink directly from you. Only one recipient at a time. The recipient's eyes may take on a slightly reddish tint while the power is active. Useful for creating a temporary bodyguard or empowering an ally.

Level 5: Earthshock

Cost: 2 Rouse | Pool: Str + Potence | Type: Combat

Stamp your foot or strike the ground with devastating force. Everyone within 10 meters (except you) must roll Dex + Athletics (Difficulty = your successes) or be knocked prone. Prone targets lose their next action getting up. The impact cracks concrete, shatters windows, and sets off car alarms. Structures may be damaged — wooden floors collapse, plaster walls crack. Deals 2 Superficial damage to everyone in the area. Cannot be used indoors above the ground floor without risking structural collapse.

Level 5: Fist of Caine

Cost: 1 Rouse | Pool: Str + Brawl (or Melee with weapon) | Type: Combat

Channel all your Potence into a single devastating melee strike. The attack deals Aggravated damage equal to your Strength + Potence (total, not additional). This can stake a vampire in one blow, crush a skull, or punch through an engine block. The attack cannot be split — it is one strike. If it misses, the power is not expended. Can be used once per scene. The sheer force of the blow produces a visible shockwave.
